app.tagline: Swing-trade signals · trend-probability model · walk-forward validated
legend.title: Validated vs Advisory
legend.validated: Validated — this asset's model beat its own benchmark out-of-sample, fees and slippage included (long side: it beats buy & hold; short side: a positive, significant Sharpe vs cash). The signal is considered actionable.
legend.advisory: Advisory — the model is trained and optimized, but has not (yet) cleared that bar. The signal stays exploratory: watch it, don't follow it.
philosophy.title: What P(win) means.
philosophy.body: The model doesn't predict a price. P(win) is the probability that, if you enter today, the target (+2 ATR) is hit BEFORE the stop (−1 ATR) within 10 trading days. The strategy rides trends with a 2:1 reward/risk, so the break-even hit rate is ~33%, not 50% — that's why the buy threshold sits at 0.35. Only assets we've already optimized are shown.
paper.tier.hint: Committed = promoted models, the real-capital track. Observation = near-miss models tracked live with no capital engaged, to gather forward evidence before trusting them.
dashboard.exportDesc: Download the full paper-trade history as CSV for external analysis.
ticker.noNews: No recent news for this ticker.
ticker.noExplain: No explanation available (no signal yet, or model not LightGBM).
ticker.noHistory: No signals recorded yet for this ticker.
ticker.trainNone: This asset hasn't been trained yet.
advice.long.head: Buy idea (long)
advice.long.body: The model sees an up-move likely to reach the target before the stop on this asset's horizon.
advice.short.head: Short-sell idea
advice.short.body: The model sees a down-move likely to reach the (mirrored) target before the stop.
advice.neutral.head: No trade today
advice.neutral.body: Neither side reaches the confidence needed to act, so the model stays on the sidelines.
advice.closeLong: The long side is close to firing — watch it over the next sessions.
advice.closeShort: The short side is close to firing — a down-signal could appear soon.
advice.promotedNote: Validated model: it beat this asset's own benchmark out-of-sample. Today it simply has no actionable edge — that's normal, not a failure.
advice.advisoryNote: Advisory model: optimized but it has not (yet) beaten this asset's benchmark, so treat any signal as exploratory.
advice.mini.long: Buy — up-move likely before the stop.
advice.mini.short: Short — down-move likely before the stop.
hood.title: Under the hood — what BeRich actually does
hood.intro: A complete, current picture of the pipeline: how data is acquired, how features are engineered, how models are trained, selected and tuned, how often everything retrains, and — most importantly — how to read the signals and where stops and targets come from. BeRich is advisory: no model currently beats buy & hold out-of-sample, and that is surfaced honestly throughout.
lang.label: Language
how.title: How BeRich works
how.intro: BeRich estimates a trend probability for each ticker — the chance that the upper barrier (target) is hit before the lower (stop) within N bars. It does not predict a price.
how.section.dataBody: Daily OHLCV bars are ingested from yfinance and cached as Parquet. The scheduler refreshes them every weekday morning.
how.section.featuresBody: 22 hand-rolled causal indicators (returns, SMAs, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, volume z-scores, cross-asset macro). All built without lookahead.
how.section.labelsBody: Triple-barrier: did the upper barrier (target = entry + k·ATR) hit before the lower (stop = entry − k·ATR) within 10 bars? 1 = win, 0 = loss.
how.section.modelBody: LightGBM baseline today. The model is only promoted if it beats both LightGBM-baseline and equal-weight buy & hold, walk-forward, out-of-sample.
how.section.signalsBody: Daily: BUY if P(win) ≥ 0.55, SELL if ≤ 0.30, NEUTRAL otherwise. Position sizing risks at most 1% of capital to the stop.
how.section.honestyBody: v0.1.0: the baseline does not beat buy & hold on this universe. Surfaced everywhere as 'advisory only'. No edge claim.
light.title: Your ideas today
light.subtitle: A plain-language read of today's signals. Advisory only — not financial advice.
light.stop: Exit if it falls to (safety stop)
light.noIdeas: No clear opportunity today — the model is staying on the sidelines.
light.howReadBody: Green = the model leans positive, grey = unsure, red = leans negative. These are probabilities, not promises. Prices are indicative.
training.title: Per-asset training
training.intro: Each asset is trained and optimized on its own, per side (long / short). A model is only promoted if it beats that asset's own benchmark out-of-sample — otherwise it stays advisory-only. This tab shows what was trained, the winning model, its guard metrics, whether a per-asset hyperparameter search ran, and when.
training.sweepHint: fills up live as the background sweep advances — refreshes every 15s
training.bothSidesNote: Long and short models both validated — separate edges on up vs down moves. The daily signal still picks only one side (or neutral), never both at once.
training.noHpoBanner: No per-asset hyperparameter search has run yet — promoted models use regularized default parameters. The deep weekend sweep (or `berich hpo --ticker ...`) populates per-asset Optuna studies.
ops.title: Machine status
ops.intro: Live view of what the box is doing in the background — GPU load, scheduled jobs, the per-asset HPO queue, and recent scheduler logs. Auto-refreshes every 5 seconds.
wallet.cardHint: Starting capital, current value, equity curve, open positions and trade history.
intro.brief.title: Your plan for today
intro.brief.body: These are the positions the robot is holding for you right now, plus the orders it plans to place tonight at 22:30 Paris. Those orders are a forecast — only the 22:30 run actually executes them.
intro.wallet.title: How your money is doing
intro.wallet.body: A simulated 10,000 € portfolio traded automatically by the robot. You see what it started with, what it's worth now, the gain or loss, and every open and closed trade. No real money is involved.
intro.opportunities.title: Every asset the robot is watching
intro.opportunities.body: The full list of assets and, for each, whether the robot would buy, short-sell or stay out — with its confidence and the entry, safety-net and target prices. Most people only need the Today page; this is the deep list.
intro.copy.title: What to copy this morning
intro.copy.body: Only the orders the robot actually executed at the last run — what to open, what to close, which stops to move. Enter your own capital to scale the amounts. Unlike Today's forecast, everything here already happened.
intro.ticker.title: One asset, up close
intro.ticker.body: The price chart, today's verdict and why the robot decided it, plus its training history for this asset.
intro.strategies.title: How the robot exits a trade
intro.strategies.body: Three ways to close a position — a fixed target, a trailing stop that follows the price up, or a mix of both. This page explains each in plain terms.
intro.training.title: How well each model is trained
intro.training.body: For every asset, where its model stands: how much tuning it got, which version won, and whether it passed the trust test. Technical, for the curious.
intro.ops.title: The machine's health
intro.ops.body: Live status of the servers and scheduled jobs that keep BeRich running — refreshed every few seconds.
onboarding.s1.title: Welcome to BeRich
onboarding.s1.body: BeRich is a robot that tells you what to buy or sell each morning. Follow the Today page and you're set.
onboarding.s2.title: “Confidence” is the robot's edge
onboarding.s2.body: Each idea comes with a confidence score — how likely the target is reached before the safety-net. Higher is stronger; it's never a guarantee.
onboarding.s3.title: Your money lives in Portfolio
onboarding.s3.body: A simulated portfolio shows how the robot's choices would have performed. No real money, no risk.
onboarding.s4.title: Want more detail?
onboarding.s4.body: Switch level — Discovery, Investor or Analyst — in the top bar. Discovery keeps it plain; Analyst shows everything.
glossary.mtm: Mark-to-market: the live gain or loss on an open position if you closed it right now. It moves with the price.
glossary.mtm.label: Live P&L
glossary.rr: Reward-to-risk: how much you aim to gain versus what you risk. 2:1 means targeting twice the size of the potential loss.
glossary.exposure: The share of your capital currently invested in open positions, versus what's held back.
glossary.winrate: The share of closed trades that ended in profit.
glossary.drawdown: The worst peak-to-trough drop of the portfolio over the period — its roughest stretch.
glossary.trailing: A stop that ratchets up as the price moves in your favour and never loosens, locking in gains.
glossary.entry: The price at which the position is opened.
strat.title: The three exit strategies
strat.intro: The model only decides WHEN to enter and on which side. How you LEAVE the trade is a separate choice — and it changes everything. BeRich offers three exit strategies, each trained, optimized and validated independently per asset, so you can pick the one that matches your philosophy with the toggle in the top bar.
strat.whatBody: Every trade has the same starting point — an entry price and an initial stop sized to risk ~1% of capital. The exit strategy decides what happens next: where you take profit, whether the stop stays put or follows the price, and when you walk away. All figures below use ATR (Average True Range), the asset's own typical daily move, so they adapt to each instrument.
strat.fixed.name: Fixed (TP / SL)
strat.fixed.philo: Discipline. Decide your exit before you enter, and stick to it.
strat.fixed.how: Two fixed barriers are set at entry and never move: a take-profit at +2 ATR and a stop-loss at −1 ATR. The trade closes at whichever is hit first, or at the 10-day time barrier. Reward/risk is locked at 2:1.
strat.fixed.example: Entry 100, ATR 2 → take-profit 104, stop 98. If the asset later runs to 120, you still sold at 104.
strat.fixed.pro: Predictable, high hit-rate, nothing to manage or second-guess.
strat.fixed.con: The upside is capped — strong trends are left on the table.
strat.fixed.best: Choppy, range-bound assets; traders who want certainty.
strat.trailing.name: Trailing (let it run)
strat.trailing.philo: Ride the trend. Never cap a winner — only exit when the move actually turns.
strat.trailing.how: No take-profit at all. The stop starts at −1 ATR; once price is +1 ATR in profit the stop follows the highest price reached, staying a wide 2.5 ATR below it and only ever ratcheting up. The trade closes when that trailing stop is hit, or at the time barrier.
strat.trailing.example: Entry 100, ATR 2. If the asset climbs to 120, the stop trails up to 115; you exit at 115 (+15%) when it reverses — versus +4% for Fixed.
strat.trailing.pro: Uncapped upside; captures the fat tail of strong trends.
strat.trailing.con: Lower hit-rate; you always give back ~2.5 ATR from the peak; more whipsaws in choppy markets.
strat.trailing.best: Strongly trending assets; a trend-following mindset.
strat.trailingtp.name: Trail+TP (secure, then cap)
strat.trailingtp.philo: Best of both: protect profit early, but still take the sure win at the target.
strat.trailingtp.how: Keeps the fixed +2 ATR take-profit AND trails the stop — but with a tight 1 ATR trail. The stop jumps to roughly breakeven as soon as you are +1 ATR up, then locks in more profit on the way to the cap. Closes at the take-profit, the trailing stop, or time.
strat.trailingtp.example: Entry 100, ATR 2 → cap at 104. Rise to 103 then reverse → the stop has ratcheted to ~101, so you exit in profit instead of falling back to the 98 stop.
strat.trailingtp.pro: Turns would-be losers (that briefly went your way) into small wins; keeps a clear target.
strat.trailingtp.con: Still caps the big trends at +2 ATR; the tight trail can exit early on a normal pullback.
strat.trailingtp.best: Most assets; a balanced, risk-managed default.
strat.paramsTitle: The numbers, and why Trail+TP trails tighter
strat.paramsBody: Take-profit +2 ATR · initial stop −1 ATR · time barrier 10 days · trail arms after +1 ATR. The trail distance differs on purpose: pure Trailing trails wide (2.5 ATR) so a normal pullback doesn't shake it out of a trend, while Trail+TP trails tight (1.0 ATR) so it locks profit BEFORE the +2 ATR target. If Trail+TP trailed as wide as the target, the target would always fire first and it would behave exactly like Fixed — the tight trail is what makes it a distinct strategy.
strat.serveBody: Each strategy is a separate model per asset, with its own hyperparameter search, and each must clear the same bar to be marked 'validated': beat that asset's own benchmark out-of-sample, fees and slippage included. So the three are genuinely measured against each other — the toggle lets you trade the philosophy you believe in, and the badges tell you which one has actually earned its keep on each asset.
ops.noAlerts: No errors or warnings — all healthy.
strategy.label: Exit strategy
strategy.hint: Pick your trading philosophy: Fixed take-profit/stop, or a trailing stop that lets winners run. The whole dashboard — signals and paper book — switches to the chosen strategy.
level.label: Detail level
level.hint.simple: The essentials only: the home page becomes your Daily brief — positions to hold, orders to open, nothing else.
level.hint.discovery: Plain-language view: just the verdict, confidence and prices, with hover help on every term.
level.hint.standard: Adds the full signals table, P(win) and the backtest — the everyday view.
level.hint.expert: Everything: per-side probabilities, guard metrics, tournament candidates and drift.
glossary.pwin.label: P(win)
glossary.pwin: Probability that the target is hit before the stop within 10 trading days — not a price prediction.
glossary.proba_long: P(win) computed for a long (buy) position on this asset.
glossary.proba_short: P(win) computed for a short (sell-to-open) position on this asset.
glossary.calibrated: The raw model score adjusted so that, say, '60%' really wins about 60% of the time historically.
glossary.meta_proba: A second model's confidence that acting on the primary signal is worthwhile; a low value can veto the trade.
glossary.sharpe.label: Sharpe
glossary.sharpe: Return earned per unit of risk (volatility). Higher is better; above 1 is good.
glossary.deflated_sharpe: A Sharpe ratio discounted for how many strategies were tried, so lucky-looking results don't fool us.
glossary.advisory: Shown for information only — this model has not proven it beats buy & hold.
glossary.promoted: This model passed every guard test (beats its benchmark out-of-sample) and is used for live signals.
glossary.drift: How much today's market data differs from what the model was trained on; large drift means retrain.
glossary.psi.label: PSI
glossary.psi: Population Stability Index — how much a feature's distribution shifted. Above ~0.2 means meaningful drift.
glossary.ks: Kolmogorov–Smirnov test — checks whether a feature's distribution changed; a low p-value flags drift.
glossary.triple_barrier: Labeling rule: a trade is a win if the target is hit before the stop within a time limit, else a loss.
glossary.tp.label: Take-profit
glossary.tp: Take-profit — the price target where the trade is closed for a gain.
glossary.sl.label: Stop-loss
glossary.sl: Stop-loss — the safety price where the trade is closed to cap the loss.
glossary.atr.label: SL/TP
glossary.atr: How the stop and target distances were set — usually from ATR, a measure of how much the price typically moves.
glossary.expreturn: Expected return per trade as a % of entry, from P(win)×reward − P(loss)×risk. GROSS is the model's raw edge; NET subtracts your round-trip fees. A signal can look good gross yet be unprofitable net — that's why both are shown.
glossary.long: Betting the price will rise (buy now, sell later).
glossary.short: Betting the price will fall (sell-to-open now, buy back later).
glossary.auc.label: AUC
glossary.auc: Area under the ROC curve — how well the model separates winners from losers. 0.5 = coin flip, 1.0 = perfect.
glossary.walk_forward: Testing the model only on dates after the data it was trained on, mimicking real out-of-sample use.
glossary.buy_hold: The simple benchmark of buying and holding the asset; a strategy must beat it to be promoted.
glossary.quantile: A range of plausible future returns (e.g. 10th–90th percentile) instead of a single point estimate.
glossary.market_neutral: A long/short basket balanced so overall market moves cancel out, isolating the model's stock-picking.
glossary.shap: A method that attributes each feature's contribution to a single prediction, explaining why the signal fired.
glossary.veto: The meta-model judged this signal not worth acting on, so it was demoted to neutral.
glossary.hpo: Hyperparameter optimization — an automated search for the model settings that work best for this asset.
